mnemonics: CPU Executes Instructions, Calculating Performance (CPU = Clock cycles * CPI * Instruction count)
Beware The Misleading MHz (Clock speed isn't everything!)
keyPoints: CPU Time = (Instruction Count * CPI) / Clock Rate
Amdahl's Law: The overall performance improvement is limited by the fraction of time the enhanced feature is used.
MIPS and MFLOPS can be misleading performance metrics because they don't account for instruction complexity.
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock rate (GHz) always means better performance, ignoring CPI and instruction count.
Using the arithmetic mean instead of the geometric mean to average normalized performance numbers, which can distort results.
Focusing on improving only the common case without considering Amdahl's Law and the limit of the non-enhanced part.
